howItWorks.intro: This calculator uses the standard amortization formula to compute your mortgage refinance calculator payment.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Actual payments may vary based on fees, insurance, and other factors.

faq.question: How is the monthly payment calculated?
faq.answer: We use the standard amortization formula: M = P[r(1+r)^n]/[(1+r)^n–1], where P is the principal, r is the monthly rate, and n is total payments.

faq.question: Should I choose a shorter or longer term?
faq.answer: Shorter terms have higher monthly payments but save significantly on total interest. Longer terms keep payments manageable but cost more overall.

faq.question: How does the interest rate affect my payment?
faq.answer: Even a 0.5% rate difference can mean thousands in total interest over the loan life. Always shop multiple lenders to get the best rate.

faq.question: Can I pay off the loan early?
faq.answer: Most loans allow extra payments toward principal. Check for prepayment penalties first. Even small extra monthly payments can save years of interest.

faq.question: What credit score do I need?
faq.answer: Requirements vary by loan type. Generally, 720+ gets the best rates. Scores below 620 may face higher rates or require alternative loan products.

whenToUse.intro: This mortgage refinance calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
